WIDESPREEAD DOWNBURST WINDS IMPACTED AREAS OF GRAND FORKS AND WALLE TOWNSHIPS. ISOLATED LARGE TREE LIMBS AND BRANCHES WERE SNAPPED ACROSS A BROAD AREA INDICATING WIDESPREAD WINDS APROACHING 60 TO 80 MPH. A NARROW CORRIDOR OF MORE EXTREME DAMAGE WAS NOTED OVER A 10 BLOCK AREA OF SOUTH CENTRAL GRAND FORKS...WITH NUMEROUS SNAPPED OR UPROOTED TREES...INDICATING PEAK WINDS NEAR 100 MPH.
MEASURED AT ND DOT SITE.

WIDESPREAD DOWNBURST WINDS IMPACTED AREAS OF GRAND FORKS AND HUNSTVILLE TOWNSHIPS. ISOLATED LARGE TREE BRANCHES AND LIMBS WERE BROKEN DOWN ACROSS A BROAD AREA INDICATING WIDESPREAD WIND GUSTS OF FROM 60 TO 80 MPH. MORE EXTREME DAMAGE WAS NOTED IN EAST CENTRAL PORTIONS OF TOWN WHERE 6 EMPTY GRAIN CARS WERE BLOWN OVER AND SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED SUGGESTING PEAK WINDS OF UP TO 105 MPH.

A TORNADO TRACKED INTERMITTENTLY TO THE EAST NORTHEAST FOR ABOUT 9 MILES...TO NEAR MICHIGAN ND BY 235 PM CDT. DAMAGE WAS CONFINED TO THE OCCASSIONAL SHELTER BELT OR GROVE ALONG THE PATH... WHERE NUMEROUS LARGE BRANCHES AND LIMBS WERE BROKEN DOWN. THE TORNADO WAS BRIEFLY VIEWED AND PHOTOGRAPHED AS IT APPROACHED MICHIGAN... BEFORE IT WAS WRAPPED IN HEAVY RAINS. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH ESTIMATED AT 100 YARDS. PEAK WIND SPEED TO 80 MPH.
DEPUTY REPORTS VERY STRONG WINDS AND TREES CRACKING. POSSIBLE DEBRIS MOTION.

GFK TOWER OFFICALS OBSERVED A BRIEF TORNADO ABOUT 3 MILES SOUTH OF THE AIRPORT TOWER. NOT MUCH OF A FUNNEL...BUT ROTATION EVIDENT IN CLOUD AND A LOT OF DUST KICKED UP. THE TORNADOLASTED LESS THAN A MINUTE AND REMAINED OVE AN OPEN FIELD. PATH LENGTH ESTIMATED AT LESS THAN ONE QUARTER MILE. PATH WITH AT LESS THAN 20 YARDS. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 70 MPH.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AND TRACKED TO THE SOUTHEAST FOR NEARLY 3 MILES TO ABOUT 2.5 NNE OF HILLSBORO BY 610 PM CDT. THIS TORNADO DESTROYED A TWO CAR GARAGE AND SNAPPED OR UPROOTED NUMEROUS TREES NORTHWEST OF THE TAFT ELEVATOR. IT CROSSED I-29 NEAR TAFT AND TORE DOWN FILLED GRAIN BINS...ELEVATOR LEGS...AND TORE THE ROOFING OFF THE OFFICE. IT THEN TORE THE ROOF OFF AND CAVED IN WALLS OF A WELL BUILT HOME EAST OF THE ELEVATOR COMPLEX. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 140 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 300 YARDS.

THIS TORNADO DEVELOPED NEAR THE POLK/NORMAN COUNTY LINE IN NORTHERN SPRING CREEK TOWNSHIP. IT INTERMITTENTLY TRAVELED SOUTH SOUTHEASTWARD TO JUST NORTHWEST OF GARY MN, EXHIBITING MULTIPLE VORTICES AT TIMES BASED ON EYEWITNESS REPORTS. HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE UPROOTED, SNAPPED, OR DAMAGED ALONG THE TRACK. A BARN WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED AT ONE FARMSTEAD.

THIS MULTI-VORTEX TORNADO WAS VIEWED FROM SEVERAL LOCATIONS NEAR THE POLK/NORMAN COUNTY LINE... BEFORE IT BECOME RAIN-WRAPPED. IT TRACKED TO THE SOUTHEAST IN A JAGGED AND INTERMITTENT FASHION FOR NEARLY 10 MILES AND ENDED ROUGHLY 1 MILE NORTHWEST OF GARY BY 1008 PM CDT. HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE UPROOTED, SNAPPED, OR DAMAGED ALONG THE TRACK. A BARN WAS DESTROYED AND POWER POLE SNAPPED AT ONE LOCALE...HOUSE ROOFS AND EXTERIOR WALLS WERE DAMAGED AT TWO OTHERS. PEAK WINDS 115 MPH. MAX WIDTH 400 YDS.

DOWNBURST WINDS CAVED IN THE SIDE OF A LARGE STEEL GRAIN ...PARTIALLY SEPERATING THE BIN FROM ITS CONCRETE PAD. SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED IN THE NEARBY SHELTER BELT. SEVERAL FARMSTEADS WERE AFFECTED...WITH A MACHINE SHED AND A POLE BARN COMPLETELY DESTROYED. ONE TRACTOR WAS TIPPED ON ITS SIDE AND A HALF DOZEN POWER POLES SNAPPED. NUMEROUS LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED IN SHELTER BELTS. ONE TRAILER HOME WAS TIPPED OVER. IN ADDITION...SEVERAL SECTIONS OF CROPLAND WERE HEAVILY DAMAGED BY WIND DRIVEN HAIL... ALL ACROSS SOUTHERN AND CENTRAL ALICETON TOWNSHIP. WIDESPREAD DOWNBURST WINDS OF FROM 80 TO 90 MPH WERE LIKELY WITH PEAK WINDS TO 115 MPH ESTIMATED AT SOME LOCALES.
